description: Fix bugs, resource leaks, and missing guards in MCP server core files identified during backend code audit. Covers: silent SDK version fallback that breaks all plugins, dead domain_all permission rules with null domain, config write mutex chain break on error, ungraceful shutdown WebSocket close, and per-write stat() in audit disk.

userStories.title: Log warning when SDK version falls back to 0.0.0
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want a visible warning when the SDK version cannot be read so that silent all-plugin-incompatibility failures are diagnosable.
acceptanceCriteria: When readFile for plugin-sdk/package.json fails, a console.error or log.warn message is emitted containing the file path and the fallback value '0.0.0'
The fallback value '0.0.0' is still used (the module must still export a string), but the operator now has a diagnostic clue
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/sdk-version.ts (27 lines total). The catch block at line 22 is empty — it should log a warning. Cannot import from logger.ts (would create a circular dep or add weight to a top-level-await module), so use console.error directly. The consequence of 0.0.0 is that ALL plugins with a declared sdkVersion fail the major.minor compatibility check in loader.ts, making every plugin a FailedPlugin with a confusing 'rebuild with newer SDK' message.

userStories.title: Fix domain_all scope creating dead permission rules when domain is null
userStories.description: As a user, I want 'Allow Always' with domain_all scope to work correctly even when the tool has no identifiable domain so that my permission decision is actually respected.
acceptanceCriteria: isSessionAllowed in state.ts handles the domain_all case when rule.domain is null: a domain_all rule with null domain matches all tool calls regardless of domain (acts as a universal allow for all tools on any domain)
Alternatively, handleConfirmationResponse in extension-handlers.ts prevents creating a domain_all rule when pending.domain is null (falls back to tool_all scope instead)

Unit tests in state.test.ts cover: domain_all with null domain matches calls with null domain, domain_all with null domain matches calls with a specific domain, domain_all with a non-null domain only matches calls with that same domain
userStories.notes:
Two files involved:

1. platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts line 215-227 — isSessionAllowed function. The domain_all case at line 222-223 checks `rule.domain !== null && rule.domain === domain` which returns false when rule.domain is null. A domain_all rule with null domain should either match everything (universal allow) or should never be created.

2. platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts line 446-462 — handleConfirmationResponse. When scope is domain_all (line 460-462), rule.tool is set to null but rule.domain keeps pending.domain which may be null.

Preferred fix: In isSessionAllowed, change the domain_all case to: if rule.domain is null, match everything (return true); if rule.domain is not null, match only when domain === rule.domain. This makes domain_all with null domain act as 'allow all tools on all domains', which is the most intuitive interpretation when there's no specific domain to anchor to.

Existing tests: platform/mcp-server/src/state.test.ts — add cases for isSessionAllowed with domain_all scope and null domain.

userStories.title: Fix config write mutex chain break on error in removeLocalPlugin
userStories.description: As a developer, I want config file writes to remain properly serialized even after a write failure so that concurrent config modifications don't corrupt config.json.
acceptanceCriteria: The .catch handler in removeLocalPlugin no longer resets state.configWriteMutex to Promise.resolve()
Instead, the catch handler allows the mutex promise to resolve normally (catch swallows the error but the promise chain continues), preserving serialization for subsequent writes

userStories.title: Allow WebSocket close frame to flush before process.exit in shutdown
userStories.description: As a Chrome extension, I want to receive a clean WebSocket close frame during server shutdown so that I can detect a graceful disconnect vs an abrupt crash.
acceptanceCriteria: After calling ws.close(), the shutdown handler waits briefly (e.g., 100-200ms via setTimeout) before calling process.exit(0)
The overall shutdown still completes quickly — the delay is a short fixed wait, not indefinite
If the WebSocket is already null or close throws, the delay is still applied (or skipped if already past)

userStories.title: Reduce per-write stat() calls in audit disk rotation
userStories.description: As a platform operator running high-throughput tool calls, I want audit log rotation to not incur a filesystem stat() on every single write so that audit logging overhead is minimized.
acceptanceCriteria: The rotateIfNeeded check is not called on every single appendAuditEntryToDisk invocation
Instead, rotation is checked at most once every N writes (e.g., every 50 or 100 writes) using a module-level counter
The counter is stored on globalThis so it survives hot reloads
Rotation still works correctly — files are rotated when they exceed 10MB, just with a delay of up to N extra entries
userStories.notes:
File: platform/mcp-server/src/audit-disk.ts (86 lines).

Line 64 calls `await rotateIfNeeded(auditPath)` before every single append. Each rotateIfNeeded call does `await stat(auditPath)` which is a filesystem syscall.

Fix: Add a globalThis-based counter (similar to the INITIALIZED_KEY pattern already in this file). Increment the counter on each write and only call rotateIfNeeded when counter % 100 === 0. Use a globalThis key like `__opentabs_audit_write_count__` so it survives hot reloads.

The 10MB file / ~200 bytes per entry = ~50,000 entries before rotation. Checking every 100 writes means we'd catch rotation within 100 extra entries (~20KB), which is negligible.

Existing test file: platform/mcp-server/src/audit-disk.test.ts — may need to account for the counter-based checking in rotation tests.
